title: Challenger Pro League

infobox.value: [Zulte Waregem](/wiki/S.V._Zulte_Waregem "S.V. Zulte Waregem")  ([2024–25](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_Challenger_Pro_League "2024–25 Challenger Pro League"))
City | Last   season   position | First season of   current spell in   top division

is the second-highest division in the [Belgian football](/wiki/Football_in_Belgium "Football in Belgium") [league system](/wiki/Belgian_football_league_system "Belgian football league system"), one level below the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League"). It was created by the [Royal Belgian Football Association](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Football_Association "Royal Belgian Football Association") in 2016, replacing the [Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division"). From the season 2016–17 until 2019–20, the competition was named ***Proximus League***, after the main sponsor [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus_Group "Proximus Group").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Football league in Belgium

Football league

Challenger Pro League

The **Challenger Pro League**, previously known as **1B Pro League** is the second-highest division in the [Belgian football](/wiki/Football_in_Belgium "Football in Belgium") [league system](/wiki/Belgian_football_league_system "Belgian football league system"), one level below the [Belgian Pro League](/wiki/Belgian_Pro_League "Belgian Pro League"). It was created by the [Royal Belgian Football Association](/wiki/Royal_Belgian_Football_Association "Royal Belgian Football Association") in 2016, replacing the [Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division"). From the season 2016–17 until 2019–20, the competition was named ***Proximus League***, after the main sponsor [Proximus](/wiki/Proximus_Group "Proximus Group").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Belgian First Division B was created in 2016 as the successor of the [Belgian Second Division](/wiki/Belgian_Second_Division "Belgian Second Division") following an overhaul of the [Belgian football league system](/wiki/Belgian_football_league_system "Belgian football league system") which saw the number of professional clubs reduced to 24 and the number of teams at the second level of the football pyramid to 8.[[1]](#cite_note-dh-1)

During Belgian Second Division era from 1973 to 2016, the second division winner and the play-off winner promote to the first division. From 2016 on, the second division winner is no longer guaranteed promotion. The league is divided in two periods of 15 games. The winners of a period compete each other in a final. If a team wins both periods, no final is played and it automatically rises to the highest level. In 2017, [Lierse](/wiki/Lierse_S.K._(1906) "Lierse S.K. (1906)") became champion of the league, but no promotion was allowed because they did not win a period. [Antwerp](/wiki/Royal_Antwerp_F.C. "Royal Antwerp F.C.") and [SV Roeselare](/wiki/K.S.V._Roeselare "K.S.V. Roeselare") played the final.

## Competition format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Competition format")]

### Until 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Until 2020")]

From its inception in 2016 until 2020, the season consisted of [two separate competitions](/wiki/Apertura_and_Clausura "Apertura and Clausura") in which all 8 teams played each other twice. The winners of both competitions faced each other in a two-legged playoff with the winner crowned champions and [promoted](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to the [Belgian First Division A](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_A "Belgian First Division A").

For the remaining teams, until 2019, the top three teams (excluding the promoted team), played together with the teams finishing in the positions 7 through 15 of the [Belgian First Division A](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_A "Belgian First Division A") in a playoff for a ticket into the [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League"). The remaining four teams played a [relegation](/wiki/Relegation "Relegation") playoff, with the last team relegating into the [Belgian First Amateur Division](/wiki/Belgian_First_Amateur_Division "Belgian First Amateur Division"). From 2019, the top six teams all took part in the Europa League playoffs (including the promoted team), with only the bottom two teams playing the relegation playoff in which they play each other five times.

### 2020 to 2022

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2020 to 2022")]

The 2020–21 and 2021–22 seasons no longer involved two separate competitions, but rather one quadruple round-robin league in which all teams play each other four times. At the end of the season, the top-ranked team was crowned champions and promoted, while the second-placed team earned a two-legged promotion playoff against the 17th-place finisher from the [Belgian First Division A](/wiki/Belgian_First_Division_A "Belgian First Division A") for a place in the top division. The team finishing in last place was relegated to the [Belgian National Division 1](/wiki/Belgian_National_Division_1 "Belgian National Division 1") (renamed from *Belgian First Amateur Division*).

### 2022 to 2023

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2022 to 2023")]

From the 2022–23 season, the league was expanded to 12 teams, as from this season U23-teams no longer play in separate competitions but rather join the regular competition.[[2]](#cite_note-2) To accommodate this expansion, the league format was changed again. Now all teams play a regular round-robin round (22 matches), after which the league splits into two halves, with the top 6 teams battling for promotion to the Belgian First Division A which only goes to the top team at the end of the season after 32 matches, while the bottom 6 also meet each other but with the goal of avoiding finishing last as this team will be relegated. Promotion/relegation playoffs are no longer organized, only the top and the bottom team at the end of the season change leagues. Furthermore, U23 teams will be able to be relegated from or promoted into the Belgian First Division B, but cannot earn promotion to the Belgian First Division A.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The league was renamed to **Challenger Pro League**.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### From 2023 onwards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: From 2023 onwards")]

From the 2023–24 season onwards, the league was expanded again to 16 teams, as from this season 4 teams U23 from top tier club will play in separate competitions but rather join the regular competition. After the end of the season, two teams will be directly promoted to Belgian Pro League, four teams will battle in the promotion play-off round with 6 matches. The team that finishes in top position at Play-off round will battle against the 2nd of the relegation play-offs in the Belgian Pro League. The two bottom teams will be directly relegated to Belgian National Division 1.

## Clubs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Clubs")]

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Members for 2024–25")]

For the 2024–25 season the following clubs will take part.

## Broadcasting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Broadcasting")]

[Proximus](/wiki/Proximus_TV "Proximus TV") broadcasts every game whilst it sponsored the league, [Telenet](/wiki/Telenet_(provider) "Telenet (provider)") broadcasts one random game every week. As of the 2020–21 season, [Eleven Sports](/wiki/Eleven_Sports "Eleven Sports") acquired the broadcasting rights to all professional league games (1A, 1B and the [Super Cup](/wiki/Belgian_Super_Cup "Belgian Super Cup")) in Belgium.[[5]](#cite_note-16)

From 2022–23 onwards, DAZN broadcast game in Challenger Pro League.

## Footnotes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Challenger_Pro_League&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Footnotes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-note1718_5-0)** Although [Tubize](/wiki/A.F.C._Tubize "A.F.C. Tubize") finished last and would have been relegated, [Lierse](/wiki/Lierse_S.K._(1906) "Lierse S.K. (1906)") went bankrupt and was removed as a team. As a result, Tubize remained in the Belgian First Division B while the matricule of Lierse was removed.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-note1920prom_6-0)** Both [Beerschot](/wiki/K_Beerschot_VA "K Beerschot VA") and [OH Leuven](/wiki/Oud-Heverlee_Leuven "Oud-Heverlee Leuven") were promoted as the First Division A was expanded to 18 teams.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-note1920EL_7-0)** Not held due to coronavirus pandemic, [Beerschot](/wiki/K_Beerschot_VA "K Beerschot VA"), [Lommel](/wiki/Lommel_S.K. "Lommel S.K."), [OH Leuven](/wiki/Oud-Heverlee_Leuven "Oud-Heverlee Leuven"), [Union SG](/wiki/Royale_Union_Saint-Gilloise "Royale Union Saint-Gilloise"), [Virton](/wiki/R.E._Virton "R.E. Virton") and [Westerlo](/wiki/K.V.C._Westerlo "K.V.C. Westerlo") had qualified.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-note1920REL_8-0)** No relegation playoffs were played due to the [coronavirus pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Belgium "COVID-19 pandemic in Belgium"), but both [Roeselare](/wiki/K.S.V._Roeselare "K.S.V. Roeselare") and [Virton](/wiki/R.E._Virton "R.E. Virton") were relegated as they were refused a professional football license due to insufficient proof of financial solvency. [Lokeren](/wiki/K.S.C._Lokeren_Oost-Vlaanderen "K.S.C. Lokeren Oost-Vlaanderen") went bankrupt and was removed as a team.
5. **[^](#cite_ref-playoff2021_9-0)** Seraing won the promotion playoff and was promoted as well.
6. **[^](#cite_ref-note2021_10-0)** Normally, [Lierse Kempenzonen](/wiki/Lierse_Kempenzonen "Lierse Kempenzonen") would have been relegated due to finishing in last place (except for [Club NXT](/wiki/Club_NXT "Club NXT") which was ineligible for promotion/relegation as a youth team). However, the general meeting of the Pro League decided on 16 March that no team would be relegated, as the [2020–21 Belgian National Division 1](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_Belgian_National_Division_1 "2020–21 Belgian National Division 1") had been canceled due to measures taken by the Belgian government against the spread of [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19") prohibiting amateur football, causing no team to be promoted from that league to fill the gap.
7. **[^](#cite_ref-playoff2122_11-0)** RWD Molenbeek lost the promotion playoff and was not promoted.
8. **[^](#cite_ref-note2122_12-0)** Although [Virton](/wiki/R.E._Virton "R.E. Virton") finished last and would have been relegated, [Excel Mouscron](/wiki/Royal_Excel_Mouscron "Royal Excel Mouscron") was refused a professional football licence and thus relegated instead.
9. **[^](#cite_ref-playoff2324_13-0)** Lommel won the promotion playoff between the Challenger Pro League teams, but lost the match against Pro League team Kortrijk and hence also did not get promoted.
10. **[^](#cite_ref-note2324_14-0)** Although [Seraing](/wiki/R.F.C._Seraing_(1922) "R.F.C. Seraing (1922)") finished in 15th place and would have been relegated, [Oostende](/wiki/K.V._Oostende "K.V. Oostende") went bankrupt and was removed as a team. As a result, Seraing remained in the Challenger Pro League.
11. **[^](#cite_ref-note2425_15-0)** Deinze went bankrupt mid-way throughout the season and dissolved, their results voided. For purposes of relegation it is counted as if finishing last.
